question.text: Which of the following correctly states the key differences between futures and forward contracts?

answer.text: Futures are standardized and exchange-traded while forwards are customized OTC contracts
Futures are OTC contracts while forwards are exchange-traded
Both futures and forwards are always physically settled
Futures require no margin while forwards require full upfront payment

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Futures are standardized, exchange-traded contracts. Forwards are customized, over-the-counter (OTC) contracts negotiated between buyer and seller. **The Trap:** Option (b) reverses the relationship. Not all contracts are physically settled. Futures DO require margin; forwards generally don't. **Cross-Exam Context:** This distinction is fundamental across all derivative exams (I, IV, VIII, XIII, XVI). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Standardization is what makes futures liquid and tradeable — anyone can buy/sell because all contract specs are identical, unlike forwards where terms are unique.
